title: Gingerbread Polish Rye Bread Kaszanka
description: A hearty, low-glycemic-friendly Polish dish featuring rye bread and slow-cooked meat, perfect for a cold winter's night.

ingredients.name: Pork blood
ingredients.notes: Available at most butcher shops or online.

instructions: Heat a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Add the pork blood and cook, breaking it up with a spoon, until it reaches 160°F (71°C).
Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute.
Add the cubed rye bread and cook, stirring occasionally, until it's lightly toasted.
Add the grated ginger and caraway seeds, and stir to combine.
Reduce heat to low and simmer, covered, for 25 minutes.
Season with salt to taste, then serve hot.
Serve with a dollop of sour cream, if desired.
